packets.body:
## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: War of the Five Kings (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Bolton victory (track_b: Result)

## Origins

After the [fall of Moat Cailin](wiki:Fall_of_Moat_Cailin), the [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn) garrison holding the ruins prevents the [northmen](wiki:North) of [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark), [King in the North](wiki:King_in_the_North), from returning north of the [Neck](wiki:Neck). The northmen march north to the [Twins](wiki:Twins) to attend the wedding of [Edmure Tully](wiki:Edmure_Tully) to [Roslin Frey](wiki:Roslin_Frey), and Robb learns at [Hag's Mire](wiki:Hag's_Mire) that [Balon Greyjoy](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy), the self-styled [King of the Isles and the North](wiki:King_of_the_Isles_and_the_North), has died at [Pyke](wiki:Pyke). Robb's plan to recover Moat Cailin involves sending [Galbart Glover](wiki:Galbart_Glover) and [Maege Mormont](wiki:Maege_Mormont) into the Neck to find [Howland Reed](wiki:Howland_Reed) and alert his [crannogmen](wiki:Crannogmen). [Greatjon Umber](wiki:Greatjon_Umber) and [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton) are to command the vanguard and rearguard, respectively, as they march from the Twins along the [causeway](wiki:Causeway) to Moat Cailin. Meanwhile, the crannogmen are to guide the rest of Robb's forces through the swamps of the Neck, so that the ironborn at Moat Cailin can be surprised by Robb from the north while the Greatjon and Roose attack from the south.(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Rasos45.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)

Robb's plan to recover Moat Cailin never develops, however, as the king and most of his men are killed at the Twins during the [Red Wedding](wiki:Red_Wedding).(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Rasos51.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) Roose Bolton, who conspired with [Walder Frey](wiki:Walder_Frey) and [Tywin Lannister](wiki:Tywin_Lannister), is named [Warden of the North](wiki:Warden_of_the_North) by the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) for his part in Robb's death.(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Rasos72.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)

When Lord Captain [Victarion Greyjoy](wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy) departs with nine-tenths of the [Iron Fleet](wiki:Iron_Fleet) for the [kingsmoot](wiki:Kingsmoot) on [Old Wyk](wiki:Old_Wyk), he leaves command of Moat Cailin to [Ralf Kenning](wiki:Ralf_Kenning).(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Raffc18.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0) Unbeknownst to the garrison, however, Victarion is ordered by the new king, [Euron Greyjoy](wiki:Euron_Greyjoy), to lead the fleet to [Essos](wiki:Essos) after the [taking of the Shields](wiki:Taking_of_the_Shields).(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Raffc29.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_12-0) Reinforcements for Ralf's garrison at Moat Cailin never show up. To make matters worse, the already small garrison is then slimmed down by guerrilla attacks from the [crannogmen](wiki:Crannogmen), whom they call [bog devils](wiki:Bog_devils);(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-5) [Harren Botley](wiki:Harren_Botley) is among the victims.(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Raffc11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

## Narrative Arc

To allow the passage of his army without taking significant casualties, Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton) assigns his legitimized bastard son, [Ramsay](wiki:Ramsay_Snow), with the task of capturing Moat Cailin from the north, where it is more vulnerable. The [Ryswells](wiki:House_Ryswell) and [Dustins](wiki:House_Dustin) surprise and burn the remaining longships of the Iron Fleet in the [Fever River](wiki:Fever_River).(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)

Ramsay Bolton gathers a large force of northerners, including [Boltons](wiki:House_Bolton), [Hornwoods](wiki:House_Hornwood), and [Cerwyns](wiki:House_Cerwyn), as well three hundred [Umber](wiki:House_Umber) spearmen and a hundred archers led by [Hother Whoresbane](wiki:Hother_Umber). Ramsay's force is to rendezvous with the Ryswells and Dustins and then take the fortress.(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd15.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-4) After Ramsay arrives, one of Lord [Rodrik Ryswell](wiki:Rodrik_Ryswell)'s sons, [Roger](wiki:Roger_Ryswell) or [Rickard](wiki:Rickard_Ryswell), states the ironborn have withstood three northern attacks already.(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-6)

Ramsay sends his servant "Reek", actually the traumatized [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy), to the castle "disguised" as Theon, the son of Lord [Balon Greyjoy](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy) and the garrison's prince. Theon is to offer the garrison terms of surrender: if the ironborn lay down their weapons and come to the Bolton camp unarmed, Ramsay will feed them and send them home to the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands). Theon is nearly struck by a crannogman's arrow while traveling to the ruins; [blood-blooms](wiki:Blood-blooms) flourish among nearby corpses.(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-7) Theon spots pale faces on the battlements of the [Gatehouse Tower](wiki:Gatehouse_Tower) and along the broken masonry atop the [Children's Tower](wiki:Children's_Tower).(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-8)

Once inside Moat Cailin, Theon discovers the garrison has been weakened by poisoned arrows and contaminated water. The cellar's vaults have flooded, so corpses are now left where they fall. All of the ironborn within the Children's Tower have died, save two cannibals eating the corpses, who were then executed by [Dagon Codd](wiki:Dagon_Codd). The nominal commander of the garrison, [Ralf Kenning](wiki:Ralf_Kenning), is now incapacitated and near death on part of a poisoned arrow. Theon puts him out of his misery by slitting his throat before offering his terms to the leaderless remaining ironborn, many of whom are from the ill-regarded [House Codd](wiki:House_Codd).(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-9)

Dagon Codd is opposed to the plan and distrusts the Boltons. However, he is killed with an axe to the head by [Adrack Humble](wiki:Adrack_Humble), who is then convinced by Theon to accept the terms on behalf of the other soldiers. Theon learns there are a mere 65 ironborn left—18 at the [Drunkard's Tower](wiki:Drunkard's_Tower) and 47 at the Gatehouse Tower—and only 58 of them are combat capable. The fortress is defensible enough that the defenders could have inflicted three to one casualties on the attackers if Ramsay tried to take the position by storm, however. The 58 able-bodied men carry out five who cannot walk, while leaving two who are near death to die, as there is no hope for them. They follow the directions and lay down their weapons before the Bolton forces.

Before allowing them to travel to the [Stony Shore](wiki:Stony_Shore) to seek transport, however, Ramsay reneges on the deal and has all 63 of the remaining ironborn flayed alive. He then puts their still-bleeding bodies upon pikes along the [causeway](wiki:Causeway).(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-10)

## Aftermath

With Moat Cailin retaken, Roose's host, consisting of 4,000 northerners (almost entirely his own Bolton men) and 2,000 [Freys](wiki:House_Frey), is able to march past the Neck and on to [Barrowton](wiki:Barrowton).(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-11)(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd32.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_13-0)

## Quotes

> [Moat Cailin](wiki:Moat_Cailin) has fallen, Reek realized then, only no one has seen fit to tell them.(wiki:Siege_of_Moat_Cailin.cite_ref-Radwd20.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-12)
>
> —thoughts of [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy)

> The [Lord of the 

...[TRUNCATED — body exceeds 8000 char cap]...

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: Greyjoy's Rebellion (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Decisive royal victory (track_b: Result)

## Origins

[Euron Greyjoy](wiki:Euron_Greyjoy) and his brother, Lord Captain [Victarion Greyjoy](wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy), led the [burning of the Lannister fleet](wiki:Burning_of_the_Lannister_fleet) anchored at [Lannisport](wiki:Lannisport) to begin [Greyjoy's Rebellion](wiki:Greyjoy's_Rebellion) on behalf of their elder brother, [Balon](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy). In response, King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) brought the [royal fleet](wiki:Royal_fleet) commanded by Lord [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon), supported by warships from the [Arbor](wiki:Redwyne_fleet), [Oldtown](wiki:Oldtown), and the [Reach](wiki:Reach), to deal with the longship threat of the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands).(wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)

## Narrative Arc

Led by Victarion, the [Iron Fleet](wiki:Iron_Fleet) found itself in the [Straits of Fair Isle](wiki:Straits_of_Fair_Isle), with the island of [Fair Isle](wiki:Fair_Isle) to their west and the mainland of the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands) to their east. Stannis's ships trapped them from the north and the south, smashing the [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn) fleet off Fair Isle.(wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle.cite_ref-Raffc1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0)(wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle.cite_ref-Radwd63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0)

*[Golden Storm](wiki:Golden_Storm)*, the ship of the youngest Greyjoy brother, [Aeron](wiki:Aeron_Greyjoy), was destroyed by Stannis's flagship, *[Fury](wiki:Fury)*.(wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle.cite_ref-Raffc1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1) Lady [Gwynesse Harlaw](wiki:Gwynesse_Harlaw)'s husband died in the battle, as did the two sons of Lord [Rodrik Harlaw](wiki:Rodrik_Harlaw).(wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle.cite_ref-Raffc11.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0)

### A Dance with Dragons

Recollection of his naval defeat bothers [Victarion Greyjoy](wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy), [Lord Captain of the Iron Fleet](wiki:Lord_Captain_of_the_Iron_Fleet).(wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle.cite_ref-Radwd63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

## Aftermath

With the threat of the [Iron Fleet](wiki:Iron_Fleet) diminished, King [Robert I Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) was able to land in the [Iron Islands](wiki:Iron_Islands) and put down [Greyjoy's Rebellion](wiki:Greyjoy's_Rebellion).(wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle.cite_ref-Rtwoiafthe_old_way_and_the_new.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1) [Aeron Greyjoy](wiki:Aeron_Greyjoy) was held prisoner by [House Lannister](wiki:House_Lannister) at [Casterly Rock](wiki:Casterly_Rock).(wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle.cite_ref-Raffc1.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)

## Quotes

> **Victarion**: Would you lesson me in warfare? I was fighting battles when you were sucking mother's milk.
>
> **Asha**: And losing battles too.
>
> —[Victarion Greyjoy](wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy) and [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy)

> The memory of [Fair Isle](wiki:Fair_Isle) still rankled in the iron captain's memory.(wiki:Sea_battle_off_Fair_Isle.cite_ref-Radwd63.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-2)
>
> —thoughts of [Victarion Greyjoy](wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy)

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: Robert's Rebellion (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Royalist victory (track_b: Result)

## Origins

With the [stormlands](wiki:Stormlands) united behind him following the [battles at Summerhall](wiki:Battles_at_Summerhall), Lord [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_Baratheon) left his brother [Stannis](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) to hold [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End) and advanced west to [Ashford](wiki:Ashford).(wiki:Battle_of_Ashford.cite_ref-Rasos36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) Ashford, located within the [Reach](wiki:Reach), was sworn to [Highgarden](wiki:Highgarden) and was likely a loyalist holding. It is possible that Robert tried to take Ashford to secure the stormlands' western flank against the threat of the [Tyrells](wiki:House_Tyrell) (who could muster the largest army in the realm), or he may have been outmaneuvered by Lord [Randyll Tarly](wiki:Randyll_Tarly).

## Narrative Arc

The battle occurred when the van of the Tyrell army under command of Lord [Randyll Tarly](wiki:Randyll_Tarly) ran into Lord [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_Baratheon)'s forces. Randyll's forces overran Robert's army, and Robert was forced to withdraw from the field before the main force of the Tyrell host had joined the battle.(wiki:Battle_of_Ashford.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)
[Lord Cafferen](wiki:Lord_Cafferen), a former [Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen) loyalist who had joined the rebels after his defeat at [Summerhall](wiki:Battles_at_Summerhall), was cut down by Randyll whilst fighting for Robert. Randyll had his head sent to King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen).(wiki:Battle_of_Ashford.cite_ref-Rasos36.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1) On the Reach side, one casualty of note was Lord Mace's cousin, Ser [Quentin Tyrell](wiki:Quentin_Tyrell).

## Aftermath

Faced with the large numbers of the main Tyrell force, Robert had no choice but to withdraw from the south and attempt to link up with his allies in the north. To this end, he force-marched his troops north towards the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands). Robert's withdrawal from the stormlands allowed the victorious Tyrell army to invade the stormlands and lay [siege to Storm's End](wiki:Siege_of_Storm's_End). However, the result of the battle was marked by [Tyrion Lannister](wiki:Tyrion_Lannister) as indecisive.(wiki:Battle_of_Ashford.cite_ref-Rasos19.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: Daughters' War (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Iron Throne (track_b: Result)

## Origins

Because the [Daughters' War](wiki:Daughters'_War) was hampering trade in the [narrow sea](wiki:Narrow_sea) in [133 AC](wiki:133_AC), Lord [Unwin Peake](wiki:Unwin_Peake), the [Hand of the King](wiki:Hand_of_the_King) to [Aegon III Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_III_Targaryen), desired to defeat [Racallio Ryndoon](wiki:Racallio_Ryndoon) and establish a presence on [Bloodstone](wiki:Bloodstone), one of the [Stepstones](wiki:Stepstones). Unwin granted command of the rebuilt [royal fleet](wiki:Royal_fleet) to his inexperienced uncle, Ser [Gedmund Peake](wiki:Gedmund_Peake), and named [Ned Bean](wiki:Ned_Bean) as Gedmund's second. The Hand instructed the eight warships and twenty cogs and galleys of Gedmund to be joined by [House Velaryon](wiki:House_Velaryon). Lord [Alyn Velaryon](wiki:Alyn_Velaryon) brought sixty war galleys, thirty longships, and more than a hundred cogs to Gedmund's fleet.(wiki:Lord_Alyn's_attack.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_war_and_peace_and_cattle_shows.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

The [Westerosi](wiki:Westeros) fleet was joined by twelve longships led by Lord [Bryndemere Tarth](wiki:Bryndemere_Tarth). While at [Tarth](wiki:Tarth), however, they learned that Racallio had allied with the [Sealord of Braavos](wiki:Sealord_of_Braavos) and the [Archon of Tyrosh](wiki:Archon_of_Tyrosh). Since [Braavos](wiki:Braavos) was the most powerful of the [Free Cities](wiki:Free_Cities), Gedmund Great-Axe and Blackbean waited for further instructions from Lord Peake. Disregarding orders, Alyn chose not to wait and instead led the Velaryon ships south toward the Stepstones.(wiki:Lord_Alyn's_attack.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_war_and_peace_and_cattle_shows.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-2)

## Narrative Arc

While the [Braavosi](wiki:Braavos) grand captain and twoscore of his captains were feasting with [Racallio Ryndoon](wiki:Racallio_Ryndoon) and the [Tyroshi](wiki:Tyrosh) envoys at Bloodstone, Lord [Alyn Velaryon](wiki:Alyn_Velaryon) attacked the unsuspecting Braavosi ships among the [Stepstones](wiki:Stepstones). The [Velaryons](wiki:House_Velaryon) captured, burned, or sank half of the Braavosi ships while they were anchored, and they fought Braavosi ships which attempted to sail. The great dromond *[Grand Defiance](wiki:Grand_Defiance)* was able to fight past six Velaryon ships, but it sank in open sea after being struck by Alyn's flagship, *[Queen Rhaenys](wiki:Queen_Rhaenys)*.(wiki:Lord_Alyn's_attack.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_war_and_peace_and_cattle_shows.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-3)

Alyn lost only three ships in his attack, including the *[True Heart](wiki:True_Heart)* of his cousin, [Daeron Velaryon](wiki:Daeron_Velaryon), and he sank more than thirty Braavosi ships. Alyn captured six galleys, eleven cogs, and eighty-nine hostages. He also claimed food, drink, weapons, currency, and an [elephant](wiki:Elephant) meant for the [Sealord](wiki:Sealord_of_Braavos).(wiki:Lord_Alyn's_attack.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_war_and_peace_and_cattle_shows.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-4)

## Aftermath

Alyn, called Oakenfist for his sinking of *[Grand Defiance](wiki:Grand_Defiance)*, was celebrated by thousands of [smallfolk](wiki:Smallfolk) when he returned to [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing). During a lavish ceremony in the throne room, Alyn Oakenfist was [knighted](wiki:Knight) by Ser [Marston Waters](wiki:Marston_Waters), the [Lord Commander of the Kingsguard](wiki:Lord_Commander_of_the_Kingsguard). Lord [Unwin Peake](wiki:Unwin_Peake) reluctantly draped a gold admiral's chain around Alyn's neck and gave him a silver replica of *[Queen Rhaenys](wiki:Queen_Rhaenys)*. King [Aegon III Targaryen](wiki:Aegon_III_Targaryen) appointed Alyn as [master of ships](wiki:Master_of_ships).(wiki:Lord_Alyn's_attack.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_war_and_peace_and_cattle_shows.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-5)

Lord Peake had been outraged at Alyn's attack, since the Westerosi soldiers meant to take [Bloodstone](wiki:Bloodstone) had been left behind with Ser [Gedmund Peake](wiki:Gedmund_Peake)'s ships at [Tarth](wiki:Tarth). [Racallio Ryndoon](wiki:Racallio_Ryndoon) remained in power and Alyn had initiated hostilities with powerful [Braavos](wiki:Braavos). Desiring to be rid of Alyn, Unwin orchestrated for the Oakenfist to sail to the [Sunset Sea](wiki:Sunset_Sea) and end the predations of Lord [Dalton Greyjoy](wiki:Dalton_Greyjoy), the Red Kraken. Alyn set sail for the west on *[Lady Baela](wiki:Lady_Baela)*, a captured Braavosi galley which he named after his wife, [Baela Targaryen](wiki:Baela_Targaryen).(wiki:Lord_Alyn's_attack.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_war_and_peace_and_cattle_shows.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-6)

Lord Peake sent Lord [Manfryd Mooton](wiki:Manfryd_Mooton) with a delegation to negotiate with the Sealord and prevent open war. Manfryd returned the elephant to the Sealord, who forgave Alyn's "unwarranted transgression" and ended the alliance with Tyrosh and Racallio. In return, the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne) paid a large indemnity to Braavos.(wiki:Lord_Alyn's_attack.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_war_and_peace_and_cattle_shows.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-7)

## Quotes

> **Unwin**: You have given us a war. **Alyn**: And an [elephant](wiki:Elephant). Pray, do not forget the elephant, my lord.(wiki:Lord_Alyn's_attack.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_war_and_peace_and_cattle_shows.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-8)
>
> —[Unwin Peake](wiki:Unwin_Peake) and [Alyn Velaryon](wiki:Alyn_Velaryon)

> We have already replaced every ship that [your boy admiral](wiki:Alyn_Velaryon) stole or sank.(wiki:Lord_Alyn's_attack.cite_ref-Rfabunder_the_regents_-_war_and_peace_and_cattle_shows.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-9)
>
> —[Sealord](wiki:Sealord_of_Braavos) to [Manfryd Mooton](wiki:Manfryd_Mooton)

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: War of the Five Kings (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Greyjoy (track_b: Result)

## Origins

[Galbart Glover](wiki:Galbart_Glover), [Master of Deepwood Motte](wiki:Master_of_Deepwood_Motte), and his brother, [Robett Glover](wiki:Robett_Glover), march south with [Robb Stark](wiki:Robb_Stark) after the arrest of his father, Lord [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark).(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Ragot53.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_5-0) After Eddard's execution, Robb is proclaimed [King in the North](wiki:King_in_the_North) at [Riverrun](wiki:Riverrun).(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Ragot71.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_6-0)

Seeking allies, Robb sends his friend, [Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy), as an envoy to his father, [Balon Greyjoy](wiki:Balon_Greyjoy), [Lord of the Iron Islands](wiki:Lord_of_the_Iron_Islands). Balon rejects Robb's offer, however, and instead intends to strike at the [north](wiki:North), which is weakly defended with northmen away fighting in the [riverlands](wiki:Riverlands) and the [westerlands](wiki:Westerlands). Balon sends his son Theon, his brother [Aeron Damphair](wiki:Aeron_Greyjoy), and [Dagmer Cleftjaw](wiki:Dagmer) to [harry the Stony Shore](wiki:Harrying_of_the_Stony_Shore) in hopes that the remaining northern lords will march away from their castles. Balon sends his daughter [Asha](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy) to seize [Deepwood Motte](wiki:Deepwood_Motte), and he sends his brother [Victarion](wiki:Victarion_Greyjoy) with the majority of the [Iron Fleet](wiki:Iron_Fleet) to seize [Moat Cailin](wiki:Fall_of_Moat_Cailin), thereby preventing the northern lords in the south from returning to their seats.(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Racok24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-1)

## Narrative Arc

[Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy) takes thirty longships of picked men and sails north around [Sea Dragon Point](wiki:Sea_Dragon_Point). She lands on the tidal flats to the north of Deepwood and seizes the castle after a month long siege with a thousand [ironborn](wiki:Ironborn).(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Racok24.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-2)(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Racok56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) [Deepwood's steward](wiki:Glover_steward) loses a leg during the fighting.(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd26.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-0)

Asha takes hostage Robett's family, including Lady [Sybelle Glover](wiki:Sybelle_Glover) and her children, [Gawen](wiki:Gawen_Glover) and [Erena](wiki:Erena_Glover), as well [Larence Snow](wiki:Larence_Snow), the Bastard of [Hornwood](wiki:House_Hornwood).(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Rasosappendix.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_8-0)

## Aftermath

Rather than follow Balon's instructions to continue raiding the Stony Shore, Theon instead [seizes Winterfell](wiki:Capture_of_Winterfell) in an attempt to compete with Asha. Having few men to hold the large castle, Theon has [Stygg](wiki:Stygg) ride to Deepwood to request aid from Asha.(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Racok50.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_9-0) She visits Theon at Winterfell, but he refuses to give up his new seat and Asha leaves him only with ten reinforcements.(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Racok56.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2) All ten abandon Theon when Ser [Rodrik Cassel](wiki:Rodrik_Cassel) approaches the castle, and Theon is soon captured by [House Bolton](wiki:House_Bolton) during the [sack of Winterfell](wiki:Sack_of_Winterfell).(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Racok66.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_10-0)

After the [Battle of the Blackwater](wiki:Battle_of_the_Blackwater), Lord [Roose Bolton](wiki:Roose_Bolton) orders Robett Glover to attack [Duskendale](wiki:Duskendale). Following the [battle at Duskendale](wiki:Battle_at_Duskendale), Roose tells Robb Stark that Robett attacked out of grief and fear for the fall of Deepwood.(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Rasos49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-1)

Asha is eventually taken prisoner by King [Stannis Baratheon](wiki:Stannis_Baratheon) during the [fight by Deepwood Motte](wiki:Fight_by_Deepwood_Motte).(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd26.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_7-1)(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Radwd42.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_11-0)

## Quotes

>
> —[Theon Greyjoy](wiki:Theon_Greyjoy) to [Asha Greyjoy](wiki:Asha_Greyjoy)

> **Robb**: [Duskendale](wiki:Battle_at_Duskendale). Grief and fear will do that to a man.(wiki:Taking_of_Deepwood_Motte.cite_ref-Rasos49.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-2)

## Identity

## Edges

- FIGHTS_IN: Robert's Rebellion (track_b: Conflict)
- DEFEATS: Rebel victory (track_b: Result)

## Origins

After King [Aerys II Targaryen](wiki:Aerys_II_Targaryen) executed Lord [Rickard Stark](wiki:Rickard_Stark) and his heir, [Brandon Stark](wiki:Brandon_Stark), in [King's Landing](wiki:King's_Landing), he sent word to Lord [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn) at the [Eyrie](wiki:Eyrie) to execute his wards, [Eddard Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark), the new Lord of [Winterfell](wiki:Winterfell), and [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon), the Lord of [Storm's End](wiki:Storm's_End). Instead, Jon sent back only defiance.(wiki:Taking_of_Gulltown.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_robert.27s_rebellion.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-0)

Ned Stark left the Eyrie to gather his [bannermen](wiki:Bannermen) from the [north](wiki:North), while Lord Arryn began to call his own vassals from the [Vale of Arryn](wiki:Vale_of_Arryn) in rebellion to the [Iron Throne](wiki:Iron_Throne). The city of [Gulltown](wiki:Gulltown) remained loyal to the [Targaryen](wiki:House_Targaryen) dynasty, however, and rather than sailing from there Ned returned to the north via the [Mountains of the Moon](wiki:Mountains_of_the_Moon), the [Fingers](wiki:Fingers), and [Sisterton](wiki:Sisterton).(wiki:Taking_of_Gulltown.cite_ref-Radwd9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-1) Jon Arryn also faced opposition from loyalists attempting to bring him down.(wiki:Taking_of_Gulltown.cite_ref-Rtwoiaf_robert.27s_rebellion.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_2-1)

## Narrative Arc

While Ned was making his way north, Lord Arryn led the rebel bannermen from the Vale against Gulltown, which was defended by [House Grafton](wiki:House_Grafton). Robert, a close friend of Ned, was the first of the rebels to gain the walls of Gulltown. Robert slew Lord [Marq Grafton](wiki:Marq_Grafton) with his own hand.(wiki:Taking_of_Gulltown.cite_ref-Radwd9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-2)

## Aftermath

The taking of Gulltown by [Jon Arryn](wiki:Jon_Arryn) allowed the Defender of the Vale to consolidate rebel control in the [Vale of Arryn](wiki:Vale_of_Arryn). [Lyn Corbray](wiki:Lyn_Corbray) won his [knighthood](wiki:Knight) fighting alongside [House Grafton](wiki:House_Grafton) at the gates of Gulltown, but he joined Lord Arryn after the battle.(wiki:Taking_of_Gulltown.cite_ref-Raffc23.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_3-0) Lord [Robert Baratheon](wiki:Robert_I_Baratheon) returned to the [stormlands](wiki:Stormlands) to raise his own bannermen, which led to the [battles at Summerhall](wiki:Battles_at_Summerhall).(wiki:Taking_of_Gulltown.cite_ref-Rasos54.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_4-0) [Ned Stark](wiki:Eddard_Stark) survived the journey across the [Bite](wiki:Bite) and raised his [northern](wiki:North) bannermen.(wiki:Taking_of_Gulltown.cite_ref-Radwd9.7B.7B.7B3.7D.7D.7D.7B.7B.7B4.7D.7D.7D_1-3)
